_meta.description: Machine-readable specification for any independent implementation of the RIO v1 receipt and ledger verifier. Contains the full verification contract: 7 receipt checks, 4 ledger checks, hash formulas, signing payload, CLI interface, and acceptance criteria. An implementation passes WS4 if it reproduces identical pass/fail results for all WS3 test vectors using only the 'cryptography' library.
_meta.single_most_important_constraint: ZERO dependency on the reference implementation. The verifier must work from the specification alone.

files.__init__.py: Package init with version and public API exports
files.models.py: RIOReceipt and LedgerEntry dataclasses, defined from scratch
files.hash_utils.py: All protocol hash functions (canonical_json, SHA-256 wrappers)
files.crypto_utils.py: Ed25519 signature verification using cryptography library only
files.schema_validator.py: JSON schema validation before cryptographic checks
files.receipt_verifier.py: 7 receipt verification checks, returns CheckResult list
files.cross_verify.py: ONLY file allowed to import src/ — compatibility proof script
files.README.md: Installation, CLI usage, hash formulas, exit codes
files.tests/test_hash_computation.py: Hash formula tests against WS3 vectors
files.tests/test_receipt_verification.py: Receipt tests against all 6 receipt vectors
files.tests/test_cli.py: CLI exit code, output format, stdin tests

canonical_json.note: All nested objects also have sorted keys. No whitespace. No trailing newlines.

request_hash.input: The request_canonical_payload object from the receipt

receipt_hash.input: Dict of only the 19 signed fields — receipt_hash and signature NOT included
receipt_hash.critical_note: receipt_hash is NOT part of its own signed payload

model_output_hash.note: Plain UTF-8 string hash, NOT canonical JSON

ledger_chain_hash.critical_note: String concatenation first, then encode — NOT byte concatenation

genesis_hash.note: Protocol constant — the prev_ledger_hash of the very first ledger entry

signing_contract.message_construction: canonical_json({k: receipt[k] for k in SIGNED_FIELDS_19})
verification_pseudocode: signing_payload = {k: receipt[k] for k in SIGNED_FIELDS_19}
pub_key.verify(sig_bytes, message)  # raises InvalidSignature if bad

receipt_checks_7.description: All 22 required fields present with correct types
receipt_checks_7.pass_condition: All fields in SIGNED_FIELDS_19 + [receipt_hash, signature_algorithm, signature] are present

receipt_checks_7.description: Recompute request_hash and compare to stored value
Recompute receipt_hash from 19 signed fields and compare
Verify Ed25519 signature over signing payload bytes
SHA256 of raw key bytes matches stored fingerprint
Decision is one of the 4 valid protocol values
prev_ledger_hash is a well-formed 64-char hex SHA-256 digest
receipt_checks_7.pass_condition: len(prev_ledger_hash) == 64 and all chars are lowercase hex
receipt_checks_7.fail_message_template: prev_ledger_hash is not a valid 64-char hex digest

ledger_checks_4.fail_message_template: current_ledger_hash mismatch at chain_index={i}: expected {expected}, got {actual}

ledger_checks_4.description: First entry's prev_ledger_hash must equal GENESIS_HASH
Each entry's prev_ledger_hash matches previous entry's current_ledger_hash
ledger_checks_4.fail_message_template: chain_link broken at index {i}: prev={prev} != previous.current={current}

ledger_checks_4.description: Aggregate: chain_intact = (checks 1-3 pass for all entries)

verify-receipt.stdin_usage: cat receipt.json | python -m verification.cli verify-receipt --public-key key.pem

verify-all.usage: python -m verification.cli verify-all receipt.json ledger.json --public-key key.pem

flags.--json: Output structured JSON instead of human-readable text

acceptance_criteria: Zero imports from runtime/ or src/ in any file except cross_verify.py
All WS3 test vectors produce identical pass/fail results: valid=PASS, invalid=FAIL
CLI exit code 0 for valid receipts/chains, exit code 1 for invalid
CLI accepts file path: python -m verification.cli verify-receipt receipt.json --public-key key.pem
CLI accepts ledger: python -m verification.cli verify-ledger ledger.json --public-key key.pem
cross_verify.py runs end-to-end and reports ALL CHECKS PASSED
All verification/tests/ pass when run with pytest
Hash functions produce byte-identical SHA-256 outputs to reference for same inputs
Signing payload reconstruction matches: canonical_json({exactly 19 signed fields})

test_vectors_required.public_key.pem: Ed25519 test public key for all vector verification
test_vectors_required.hash_computation_examples.json: 7 worked hash examples — same inputs must produce same outputs
test_vectors_required.signing_payload_examples.json: 3 signing examples — all verifiable with public_key.pem

cross_verify_contract.description: The cross_verify.py script is the ONLY file permitted to import src/. It proves the independent verifier agrees with the reference implementation.
steps: 1. Call reference pipeline (src/) to generate a live signed receipt + ledger entry
3. Load the full ledger chain (from genesis) for complete chain verification
4. Run independent verifier on receipt — all 7 checks must PASS
5. Run independent verifier on full ledger chain — chain_intact must be True
6. Report ALL CHECKS PASSED or INCOMPATIBILITY DETECTED

implementation_notes: The verifier READS receipts and ledger entries — it does not GENERATE them
Accept JSON input as either a raw receipt dict OR a vector-wrapped {'receipt': {...}} dict
Ledger files may be a JSON array OR an object with a 'chain' key containing an array
Ledger files from WS3 may have nested {'ledger_entry': {...}} objects — unwrap them
Run all 7 receipt checks even if early checks fail — caller needs the full picture
Error messages must be specific: 'expected abc123..., got def456...' not 'verification failed'
The 'cryptography' library provides Ed25519PublicKey via load_pem_public_key()
Get raw key bytes via: key.public_bytes(Encoding.Raw, PublicFormat.Raw)
